title: Homosexuality nature or nurture - Request.  I say nature.

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that homosexuality is primarily nature, not nurture, though he believes unhealthy societal conditions (overcrowding, overpopulation) can increase it as a biological corrective mechanism. He shares personal anecdotes, estimates prevalence rates by sex, and touches on spirituality, ancient history, and future social trends.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: primarily nature in a healthy society; nurture/conditioning plays a role in overcrowded or sex-segregated environments
roughly 1% of men are gay or bisexual; roughly 30% of women are gay or bisexual
may serve a natural species-saving function to prevent overpopulation
believes people can be conditioned into homosexuality in sex-segregated settings like prison or war
serves a higher spiritual purpose by bridging the sexes and expanding open-mindedness
not sure strict monogamy will persist as society evolves
women are not less intelligent, just conditioned to be closed-minded; younger generations will improve
